task_description: Compress the selected files into a ZIP archive and send it as an email attachment to the specified recipient.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X placed over a plain dark empty area, not on any visible icon, button, text label, file name, or editor content. In the full-screen view, Visual Studio Code is open in a dark theme with the Explorer on the left showing a folder named FILE1, a subfolder named test, and files including package.json and text1.txt through text4.txt. The main editor displays package.json. The red X sits in the central editor pane over blank space below the JSON content, indicating the click is on empty editor background rather than on a specific object. No context menu is visible yet.
caption.think: The user is right-clicking a blank area inside the editor pane, likely to open the editor's context menu for general editing or view-related options rather than actions on a specific file or code token.
caption.action: Right-click the empty dark background area in the editor pane.
caption.expectation: A context menu should appear for the editor background, typically containing editing and view options such as command palette or actions, cut/copy/paste, select all, formatting, and editor-related commands rather than file-specific object actions.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on a context-menu row labeled “在文件资源管理器中显示”, with the shortcut text “Shift+Alt+R” visible on the right. This is a menu command, not blank space. In the full-screen view, Visual Studio Code is open in a dark theme with the Explorer on the left; the file “package.json” is selected under the folder structure, and a right-click context menu for that file is open. The highlighted row under the X is the command to reveal the selected file in the system file manager.
caption.think: The user likely wants to open the location of the selected package.json file in the operating system's file explorer from VS Code.
caption.action: Click the context-menu item “在文件资源管理器中显示” (Show in File Explorer).
caption.expectation: The context menu should close, and a system File Explorer window should open to the folder containing the selected package.json file, with that file likely highlighted or visible.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a Windows File Explorer window in details view with the file row “package.json” highlighted in light blue. The row shows the file name, type “JSON File,” and size “1 KB,” indicating it is the currently selected item. There is no text caret or editable text field visible in the crop. In the full-screen view, File Explorer is the active window opened to the folder “file1,” with the breadcrumb path leading through the user desktop folder. Other items such as “.git,” “test,” and several text files are listed. Visual Studio Code remains visible behind the Explorer window. The action is a keyboard shortcut, so it applies to the current selection in the file list rather than typing into a text entry area.
caption.think: The user likely wants to select all items in the current File Explorer folder, expanding the selection from just “package.json” to every file and folder shown.
caption.action: Press Ctrl+A to select all items in the current File Explorer folder.
caption.expectation: All files and folders in the visible File Explorer list should become selected, not just “package.json.”

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the file row labeled “text2.txt,” specifically over its Type column value “文本文档,” so the click target is that selected file row rather than blank space. In the full-screen view, a Windows File Explorer window for the folder “file1” is active, and all seven items in the file list are highlighted in light blue, indicating they are all currently selected. No context menu is visible yet. Visual Studio Code is open behind the Explorer window.
caption.think: The user is right-clicking directly on one of the selected files within a multi-selection, likely to open the File Explorer context menu for the selected items and access file operations rather than clicking empty space or a toolbar area.
caption.expectation: A File Explorer context menu should appear for the current selection, showing file/object actions for the selected items such as open, cut, copy, delete, rename, compress, properties, and other item-related commands rather than general blank-area view or sorting options.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the submenu item labeled “添加到 'file1.zip'”. This is a menu command inside the 360压缩 submenu, not blank space. In the full-screen view, Windows File Explorer is open to the folder “file1,” all seven items in the file list remain selected, a context menu is open for the selected files, and its 360压缩 submenu is expanded on the right. The X is on the command to add the selected items into a ZIP archive named “file1.zip.”
caption.think: The user wants to compress all currently selected files and folders into a single ZIP archive using the 360 compression submenu option.
caption.expectation: The submenu and context menu should close, and the compression process should start, creating a new ZIP archive named “file1.zip” in the current folder.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the Google Chrome taskbar icon, the circular red-yellow-green-blue browser logo. It is a taskbar UI control, not a file or blank area. In the full-screen view, File Explorer is open to the folder “file1,” and a new archive named “file1.zip” is visible in the file list, indicating the compression just completed. Visual Studio Code is open behind Explorer, and the Windows taskbar is visible along the bottom with several pinned apps including File Explorer, Chrome, Steam, and VS Code.
caption.think: The user likely wants to switch away from File Explorer and open Google Chrome next, probably to continue the workflow in a web browser after confirming the ZIP file was created.
caption.action: Click the Google Chrome icon on the taskbar.
caption.expectation: Google Chrome should open or come to the foreground, making the browser window active instead of File Explorer.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the bookmark bar item labeled “QQ邮箱,” with a round multicolor icon to its left. This is a browser UI control on the bookmarks bar, not page content or blank space. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open on a new tab page with the Google logo, search box, shortcut tiles, and the bookmarks bar visible below the address bar. The X is on the “QQ邮箱” bookmark near the left side of the bookmarks bar.
caption.think: The user likely wants to open the QQ Mail website by activating its saved bookmark from the Chrome bookmarks bar.
caption.action: Click the “QQ邮箱” bookmark on the bookmarks bar.
caption.expectation: Chrome should navigate away from the new tab page and load the QQ Mail website in the current tab.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the blue rectangular button labeled “写信,” with a small paper-and-pencil compose icon at its left. This is a clickable UI control in the left sidebar of the QQ Mail interface. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is displaying the QQ邮箱 webmail inbox, with the message list in the center and the left navigation panel visible; the X is on the compose-mail button near the top of that sidebar.
caption.think: The user likely wants to start creating a new email message from the QQ Mail inbox.
caption.action: Click the blue “写信” compose button in the left sidebar.
caption.expectation: A new message composition interface should open, typically showing fields for recipients, subject, and message body.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered inside the blank white recipient input area next to the label “收件人,” just below the top row of mail actions like “发送,” “预览,” and “附件.” This is not a toolbar button or icon; it is a text-entry field in the compose form. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is displaying the QQ邮箱 message composition page, with the left sidebar, the large email body editor below, and a contacts panel on the right. The X is on the recipient field at the top of the draft.
caption.think: The user likely wants to place the text cursor in the recipient field so they can enter one or more email addresses for the new message.
caption.action: Click the blank “收件人” recipient field.
caption.expectation: The recipient field should become active with a text caret, indicating it is ready for typing recipient addresses.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The recipient text entry area labeled “收件人” at the top of the QQ Mail compose form is active, and the email address “1348952181@qq.co” is currently visible being entered in that field, indicating text is being appended into the blank recipient line rather than typed into the subject or message body. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open to the QQ邮箱 compose page, with action buttons such as “发送,” “预览,” and “附件” above, a blank “主题” subject field below the recipient line, the large message editor area underneath, and a contacts sidebar on the right.
caption.think: The user is entering the recipient’s email address into the To field so the message can be addressed to that contact.
caption.action: Type "1348952181@QQ.COM" into the “收件人” recipient field.
caption.expectation: The full recipient email address should appear in the recipient field, and the compose form should remain ready for the next step, such as entering a subject or message body.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the gray button labeled “附件,” which has a paperclip icon and a small dropdown arrow beside it. This is a toolbar control in the top action row of the QQ Mail compose page, next to buttons such as “预览” and “超大附件.” In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open to the QQ邮箱 message composition window: the recipient line already contains “1348952181,” the subject field “主题” is still blank, the message body editor is empty, and the contacts list is visible on the right.
caption.think: After entering the recipient, the user likely wants to add a regular file attachment to the email before continuing with the subject or body.
caption.action: Click the “附件” paperclip attachment button in the top compose toolbar.
caption.expectation: A file selection dialog or attachment picker should open immediately, allowing a file to be chosen and attached to the email.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the folder named “file1” in the left navigation pane of the Windows Open file dialog. The folder row is highlighted, with a yellow folder icon followed by the text label “file1,” indicating this is a selectable navigation item rather than a blank area. In the full-screen view, the Open dialog is in front of Chrome and is currently browsing a folder path under Data (D:) > 计算机图形学 > src. The main file list on the right displays files such as “bricks.png,” “nyra.obj,” “nyra.png,” “obj.js,” “project2.html,” “project2.js,” and “teapot.obj,” while the QQ Mail compose page remains visible in the background.
caption.think: The user is likely choosing the “file1” folder in the dialog’s left pane to navigate or confirm that location while selecting a file to attach to the email.
caption.action: Click the “file1” folder in the left navigation pane of the Open dialog.
caption.expectation: The “file1” folder should become selected in the dialog, updating or confirming the current location so its contents are shown in the main file list.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the row for “file1.zip,” which has a WinRAR-style stacked-books archive icon and the type text indicating it is a ZIP compressed file. The row is already highlighted in light blue, showing this file item in the file list is the exact click target. In the full-screen view, this is the Windows Open dialog in front of Chrome’s QQ Mail compose page, inside the folder “file1,” with files such as .git, test, package.json, text1.txt, and text2.txt visible. The target is an object in the file list, not a toolbar control or blank area.
caption.think: The user is selecting the archive file to attach it to the email, likely confirming or reselecting the intended file before opening it.
caption.action: Click the “file1.zip” file item in the Open dialog’s file list.
caption.expectation: The “file1.zip” item should remain selected, confirming the file choice and preparing it to be opened or attached from the dialog.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the blue-highlighted button labeled “打开(O)” in the lower-right area of the Windows file chooser. This is a standard dialog control button, not a file item or blank space. In the full-screen view, the Open dialog is in front of Chrome’s QQ Mail compose page, and the file “file1.zip” is selected in the file list with its name also populated in the filename field, indicating the dialog is ready to confirm that attachment choice.
caption.think: The user has already selected the ZIP file and now intends to confirm the selection so the file is attached to the email.
caption.action: Click the “打开(O)” button in the Open dialog.
caption.expectation: The Open dialog should close, and the selected “file1.zip” file should begin attaching to the QQ Mail compose window.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the blue button labeled “发送,” with a paper-plane icon on its left. This is a toolbar control button at the top of the QQ Mail compose form, not a blank area or canvas object. In the full-screen view, Chrome is open to the QQ Mail compose page with recipient “1348952181,” subject “file1,” and an attached file “file1.zip (25KB)” already listed below the message body area, indicating the email is ready to be sent.
caption.think: The user has finished attaching the ZIP file and now intends to send the composed email to the listed recipient.
caption.expectation: The mail should be submitted immediately, typically leaving the compose view and showing a send-success confirmation or returning to the mailbox after sending.
